2606.27657.title: Reconstructing the Developmental Trajectory of Adipocytes in Human Adipose Tissue Using Single-Cell RNA Sequencing
2606.27657.paper_summary: Obesity is a global health crisis associated with metabolic disorders such as type 2 diabetes and cardiovascular disease. This study employed single-cell RNA sequencing to reconstruct the developmental trajectory of human adipocytes from adipose tissue samples. Our analysis identified 15 transcriptionally distinct cell clusters, including 7 transitional states, revealing the dynamic process of adipocyte differentiation. We detected 16 functionally active signaling pathways mediating cellular communication between adipocytes and their progenitors. Among these, insulin-like growth factor (IGF) and fibroblast growth factor (FGF) pathways emerged as the most prominent networks, showing consistent activity across differentiation stages (p<0.05). The study revealed depot-specific differences, with visceral adipocytes undergoing additional extracellular matrix remodeling absent in subcutaneous differentiation. Spatial analysis further showed that IGF signaling was particularly active in perivascular niches, while FGF activity dominated in mature adipocyte zones. These results provide the first comprehensive map of human adipocyte development, highlighting IGF and FGF pathways as potential therapeutic targets. The identified signaling networks offer new insights for developing interventions to promote healthy adipose expansion or inhibit pathological fat accumulation. This work advances our fundamental understanding of adipose tissue biology while providing clinically relevant data for metabolic disorder treatments.
2606.27657.paper_summary_zh: 肥胖是一個全球健康危機，與代謝疾病如第二型糖尿病和心血管疾病相關。本研究採用了單細胞RNA測序技術，重建了來自脂肪組織樣本的人類脂肪細胞的發展軌跡。我們的分析識別了15個轉錄上不同的細胞群，包括7個過渡狀態，揭示了脂肪細胞分化的動態過程。我們檢測到16條功能活躍的信號通路，介導脂肪細胞與其前體之間的細胞通信。在這些通路中，類胰島素生長因子（IGF）和成纖維細胞生長因子（FGF）通路成為最突出的網絡，顯示出在分化階段中的一致活性（p<0.05）。研究揭示了庫存特異性差異，內臟脂肪細胞經歷了在皮下分化中缺失的額外細胞外基質重塑。空間分析進一步顯示，IGF信號在血管周圍的微環境中特別活躍，而FGF活性則主導於成熟脂肪細胞區域。這些結果提供了人類脂肪細胞發展的首個綜合地圖，突顯了IGF和FGF通路作為潛在的治療靶點。所識別的信號網絡為開發促進健康脂肪擴張或抑制病理性脂肪積累的干預措施提供了新的見解。這項工作推進了我們對脂肪組織生物學的基本理解，同時為代謝疾病的治療提供了臨床相關數據。
2606.27657.author: Weny S. M Sitinjak et.al.
2606.27657.authors: Weny S. M Sitinjak, Humasak Tommy Argo Simanjuntak
